signals.idea: Light megaplan: Consolidate all TimelineData construction into a single canonical builder function. Currently there are multiple code paths that build TimelineData objects (buildDataFromCurrentRegistry, buildTimelineData, patchRegistry hand-assembly) and they don't all go through migration/deduplication. The fix is to make one function that guarantees consistent tracks/clips/config, and route all construction through it. This will eliminate the duplicate key bugs (V3 tracks, cascading -dup- clip IDs) at the structural level.

unresolved_flags.concern: Timeline data migration: The plan does not actually achieve its stated "migrate exactly once" invariant on the async load path. `buildTimelineData()` already migrates before resolving, but the shared `resolveTimelineConfig()` helper migrates again internally, and the plan never updates that helper.

Timeline data assembly: The proposed `assembleTimelineData()` centralizes the outer `TimelineData` object literal, but it still leaves `resolvedConfig` and `assetMap` derivation split across multiple callers. That means the plan does not fully deliver on the stated goal of one canonical construction path that guarantees consistent tracks/clips/config by construction.

Timeline data validation: The plan refactors the two sync construction paths most closely tied to the duplicate-ID bug (`buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` and `patchRegistry`), but it only proposes existing tests plus manual smoke checks. There is no automated regression coverage for the exact paths being changed.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 4.25. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 3.

items: Only one function (`assembleTimelineData`) stamps out TimelineData objects — all other construction paths delegate to it
No TimelineData object literal `{ config: ..., rows: ..., ... }` exists outside `assembleTimelineData` (except test mocks if deliberately excluded)
`migrateToFlatTracks` is called exactly once per construction path (no double-migration)
All existing video-editor tests pass without modification (other than test helper updates)
No duplicate-key React warnings when loading V3 timeline data
No cascading `-dup-` clip ID suffixes after registry patches

The shared resolver then re-runs `migrateToFlatTracks(config)` in [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts:77] and [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts:82].

That means the plan does not fully deliver on the stated goal of one canonical construction path that guarantees consistent tracks/clips/config by construction.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Today the sync paths each hand-build `resolvedConfig` and related derived fields in [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-save-utils.ts:30] and [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts:335]. The plan's Step 4 and Step 5 examples keep that manual assembly pattern and only swap the final return object for `assembleTimelineData(...)`. That reduces duplication, but it does not make derivation canonical.

unresolved_flags.evidence: The repository has production logic in [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-save-utils.ts:19] and [/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts:303], but a repo-wide test search found no tests invoking `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry(...)`, `patchRegistry(...)`, or `useTimelineSave(...)`.
